Claude Opus 4.7 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved multi-step task performance and more reliable agentic execution.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.2 has been released. It adds support for Kotlin 2.3.20 and includes other unspecified updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enable Copilot cloud agent via custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent can now be enabled selectively for specific organizations using custom properties. Before this change, enterprise admins and AI managers could only turn the agent on or off across all organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of lift reminder of HBv2/HC-Series/NP-Series Azure Virtual Machine in Azure Batch Pool
item.description:
Azure Batch will retire support for HBv2-series, HC-series, and NP-series VMs in Batch pools on May 31, 2027. The notice applies to these VM sizes only when used in Azure Batch pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Encrypt Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks with Cross Tenant Customer Managed Keys
item.description:
Cross-tenant customer-managed keys for Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks are now generally available. Managed disks can now be encrypted with a CMK stored in an Azure Key Vault in a different Microsoft Entra tenant.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps
item.description:
The .NET MAUI Map control in .NET MAUI 11 now supports pin clustering, automatically grouping nearby pins into cluster markers. The post also covers configuring separate clustering groups and handling cluster tap events on Android and iOS.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Incident response for AI: Same fire, different fuel
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post on incident response for AI, focusing on how existing IR practices still apply while AI-specific telemetry, tools, and skills are needed. It frames AI incidents as similar to traditional response work but with different signals and operational needs.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Windows Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description:
Azure Site Recovery now supports Azure-to-Azure replication and disaster recovery for Windows Azure VMs that use NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families. Supported examples include Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Announcing: Minimum billable object size for cooler storage tiers
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage and ADLS will now use a 128 KiB minimum billable object size for the cool, cold, and archive access tiers. Objects smaller than 128 KiB on those tiers will be billed as 128 KiB.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for private registries configured at the organization level. This removes the need to store long-lived credentials as repository secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deployment context in repository properties and alerts
item.description:
GitHub added deployment context to repository properties and security alert pages. Two new built-in repository properties, deployable and deployed, now expose this information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework April 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of April 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. It does not include specific version details or change items in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 3 is now available!
item.description:
Microsoft announced .NET 11 Preview 3, covering updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and container images. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub added the ability to link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues. The feature is in public preview and is meant to connect security remediation work with existing planning and tracking workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot and code scanning: Org-level private registries
item.description:
GitHub now lets organizations configure multiple private registries at the org level for Dependabot and code scanning, which is useful for setups that use more than one internal package feed. Previously, org-level settings supported only one private registry per ecosystem.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description:
GitHub says it is rolling out secret scanning updates that improve detection coverage, APIs, and related workflows. The post says these changes are part of ongoing secret scanning product improvements, but the provided excerpt does not include具体 details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Smart Tier (Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage)
item.description:
Smart Tier for Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage is now generally available in nearly all zonal public cloud regions. The release excludes Israel Central, Qatar Central, and UAE North at launch.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: SBOM exports are now computed asynchronously
item.description:
SBOM exports from repository dependency graph pages and the new API endpoints are now computed asynchronously. This changes export handling from a synchronous action to an async one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Model selection for Claude and Codex agents on github.com
item.description:
GitHub.com now lets users select the model when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent runs. The feature is available for these agents, similar to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Improvements to standard findings in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality has updated its standard findings experience in public preview to make repository-wide navigation and triage easier. The changelog notes improvements focused on finding reliability and related issues, but does not provide detailed feature specifics in the excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure File Sync in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure File Sync is now generally available in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central. The service supports tiering data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid scenarios and migration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports data residency in US and EU regions, keeping inference processing and related data within the selected geography. It also adds FedRAMP compliance for US government customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added a new “Fix with Copilot” button on github.com for resolving merge conflicts, powered by Copilot cloud agent. The feature lets users fix conflicts from a comment flow in three clicks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Managed identity support for graphical session recording
item.description:
Azure Bastion graphical session recording in public preview now supports managed identities for writing to storage accounts. This removes the need to use other credentials for storage access when configuring session recording.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now generally available in Azure Government (US). It provides a fully managed way for U.S. government customers and partners to migrate large-scale file data into Azure Government cloud environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Monitor AKS applications with OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor
item.description:
Azure Monitor now supports monitoring applications running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with OpenTelemetry. The preview includes autoinstrumentation to deploy the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distribution to workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Granular Encryption-in-Transit Controls for SMB and NFS on Azure Files
item.description:
Azure Files now lets you configure encryption-in-transit separately for SMB and NFS at the storage account level. This enables protocol-specific security policies instead of a single shared setting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description:
Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027. Customers are advised to move to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for AKS
item.description:
AKS Public Preview adds StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for managed and BYO VNets. It provides zone-redundant outbound connectivity, up to 100 Gbps outbound throughput, and IPv6 outbound support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports remote control via copilot --remote, allowing users to monitor and steer a running CLI session from web and mobile. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description:
GitHub is enforcing new usage limits for Copilot Pro+ and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from that plan. The change is intended to address high-concurrency and heavy-usage patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description:
GitHub is renaming Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and updating the data schema to reflect that change. Copilot usage metrics will now aggregate active user counts for the cloud agent under the new naming.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description:
GitHub Actions workflow runs are now limited to 50 reruns per workflow. Attempts to rerun a workflow beyond that limit will fail with a failed check suite.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include CLI activity in the overall totals and feature breakdowns. Previously, CLI metrics were only available separately in a standalone totals_by_cli section.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Event Grid as a destination for Stripe events now in Public Preview
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is now in public preview as a destination for Stripe events. Developers can route Stripe events into Event Grid to build event-driven architectures without managing webhook infrastructure or custom brokers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description:
GitHub says the validation tools used by Copilot cloud agent are now 20% faster. The tools include CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | March 2026
item.description:
March 2026 Foundry updates include Agent Service GA with private networking, new GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4 Mini models, Priority Processing, Phi-4 Reasoning Vision, SDK 2.0 GA across major languages, additional Fireworks AI and NVIDIA Nemotron models, and third-party guardrails from Palo Alto and Zenity.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description:
GitHub added an Ask Copilot entry point in security assessment results, letting organization admins and security managers get contextual explanations and next-step guidance directly from secret risk assessment and Code Security risk assessment views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: Foundry Local is now Generally Available
item.description:
Microsoft says Foundry Local is now generally available for running AI inference on-device. The announcement emphasizes local deployment, privacy, and no per-token costs.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: The agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decade
item.description:
Microsoft describes a future SOC model where autonomous defense handles machine-speed response, agents provide context and coordination, and human analysts focus on judgment, risk, and outcomes.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description:
GitHub moderators can now mark hidden comments as Low Quality from the Hide comment menu. The option is available on issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description:
GitHub Notifications now includes a new “Sort by” control with two options: “Newest to oldest” and “Oldest to newest.” The default behavior remains “Newest to oldest.”

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description:
GitHub now shows repository member role labels inline in the pull request list view for public repositories. Labels such as First-time contributor, Contributor, and Member appear directly in the list.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description:
GitHub added release tracking details to the issue sidebar, configurable default values for project fields, and faster navigation between related issues.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
item.description:
Microsoft reports on Storm-2755, a financially motivated threat actor targeting Canadian employee accounts to access profile data and redirect salary payments to attacker-controlled accounts. The post is based on observations from Microsoft Incident Response – Detection and Response Team (DART).

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Intent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential risk
item.description:
Microsoft says a severe Android intent-redirection flaw in a widely used third-party SDK exposed sensitive user data across millions of apps, including Android wallets. The post explains the issue, its risk, and recommends updating affected SDKs to reduce exposure.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: aks: Release 2026-04-02
item.description:
AKS release 2026-04-02 adds new Kubernetes patch versions, preview and GA feature updates, component bumps, and several bug fixes/security remediations. It also announces future retirements for Ubuntu 22.04, a kubelet certificate rotation disable tag, and Teleport (preview).

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: github: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes two new merge metrics for Copilot-reviewed pull requests, extending the pull request throughput and cycle-time metrics added in February. These metrics track PRs reviewed by Copilot from the coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out updates to secret scanning alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows. The changes are aimed at improving the developer experience for secret scanning features.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports Copilot cloud agent beyond pull request workflows, letting users continue work away from their desk. The update adds the ability to ask Copilot to research and code from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhancements to maintenance notifications in Azure Service Health
item.description:
Azure Service Health now provides regional, consolidated maintenance notifications for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. Instead of separate notices for each server, customers receive one notification per region for upcoming maintenance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PgBouncer 1.25.1 support in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description:
PgBouncer 1.25.1 is now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server. It is offered as a built-in connection pooling feature to help manage idle and short-lived connections with low overhead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Files assessments are now available using Azure Migrate
item.description:
Azure Migrate now supports public preview assessments for Azure Files. It can discover on-premises SMB and NFS file shares and provide migration planning for Windows and Linux shares to Azure Files.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Observability in AKS Namespace and Workload Views
item.description:
AKS Namespace and Workload views now include observability data from Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus. This adds cluster and workload health, troubleshooting, and resource utilization details directly in those views.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description:
Organization admins and security managers can now run a free Code Security risk assessment for their organization. It summarizes vulnerabilities by severity, rule type, and related categories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description:
GitHub CLI’s Linux package repositories now use an updated PGP keyring that includes both the existing signing key and a replacement key. The change is meant to support package signing continuity and will matter if you refresh or verify repository keys.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description:
VS Code moved to weekly stable releases, and this changelog covers Copilot-related updates in VS Code versions 1.111 through 1.115 shipped in March and early April 2026. The post says the highlights include Autopilot for fully automated workflows, but the provided excerpt does not include the detailed release items.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift adds NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU support
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) now supports NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU-based Azure VM SKUs. The update targets large-scale AI, machine learning, and HPC workloads on the managed OpenShift service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description:
The post introduces GitHub Copilot Modernization and focuses on the assessment report as the source of truth for cloud modernization work. It frames the report as part of the migration process and positions it as the primary reference for modernization planning.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Disable HTTP proxy in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service now supports disabling HTTP proxy settings in an existing cluster. This helps administrators remove or change proxy configuration as network requirements evolve without needing disruptive cluster changes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports MCP resource triggers for MCP servers hosted on Functions. This lets developers expose resources directly, in addition to tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now supports generally available Pod CIDR expansion for overlay-based clusters. This lets teams increase the pod IP range when clusters run out of addresses, avoiding cluster rebuilds.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement
item.description:
ASP.NET Core 2.3 will reach end of support on April 7, 2027. Microsoft says users should plan to upgrade applications to a currently supported .NET version.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security alerts can now be prioritized using runtime context from Dynatrace, including deployed artifacts and runtime risk in Kubernetes environments. This adds environment-aware signal to help rank alerts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports connecting your own model provider (BYOK) or using fully local models instead of GitHub-hosted model routing. The change gives CLI users more control over where models run and which providers they use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description:
Dependabot alerts can now be assigned to AI coding agents for remediation, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex. This is intended for vulnerabilities that require code changes beyond a simple dependency version update.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem via Nix flakes. You can add nix as a package ecosystem in dependabot.yml and Dependabot will monitor flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when updates are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: SOHO router compromise leads to DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks
item.description:
Microsoft says Forest Blizzard, a threat actor linked to the Russian military, is compromising insecure home and small-office routers and changing their settings to support malicious infrastructure. The activity includes DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests can now be fixed in bulk from the Files changed tab. The update lets users apply multiple security alert suggestions at once instead of handling them individually.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
item.description:
npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI as an OIDC provider for publishing packages to npm. Maintainers can authenticate from CircleCI workflows without stored credentials, alongside existing support for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot code review activity and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can use these metrics to see how users engage with Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description:
Network Security Perimeter support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. It lets you define a logical network boundary around Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources to block unauthorized public access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description:
Microsoft describes a new device code phishing campaign that uses AI and end-to-end automation to generate live authentication codes on demand. The result is more scalable account compromise, higher success rates, and longer post-compromise access.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher now offers rule impact analysis in public preview. It lets you preview how security admin rules will affect your environment before applying them, including validating behavior and identifying possible conflicts.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description:
Microsoft says Storm-1175 is running fast-moving Medusa ransomware campaigns that exploit recently disclosed vulnerabilities in web-facing systems for initial access, data theft, and ransomware deployment. The actor uses Medusa ransomware, also referred to as Gaze.exe.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level runner controls for Copilot cloud agent development environments. Copilot cloud agent tasks now start on GitHub Actions, and orgs can control which runners are used instead of relying only on the default GitHub-hosted runner.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini across all GitHub Copilot experiences on April 1, 2026. The change applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent. The built-in agent firewall can now be configured centrally to control internet access and help reduce prompt injection and data exfiltration risk.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it creates. These commits show up as Verified on GitHub, indicating they were made by the agent and not altered after signing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now in public preview. It provides building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities into applications, workflows, and platform services.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization reports now include per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity. This completes CLI metrics coverage across enterprise-, user-, and organization-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description:
GitHub Actions added entrypoint and command overrides for service containers. The update also includes new security features: OIDC custom properties and VNET failover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description:
C# 15 adds preview support for union types, letting you define a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. The post also notes this fits into a broader exhaustiveness roadmap.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: vscode: Insiders (version 1.111)
item.description:
The provided content only includes the title and a link, without any release note details to summarize.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: azure: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description:
Python apps hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, app configuration and content remain, but the apps will stop running.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description:
AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will be retired in March 2027. Microsoft advises moving all new and existing blob storage account deployments to Standard endpoints.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description:
Microsoft says threat actors are increasingly using generative AI to improve cyberattacks, including much higher phishing click-through rates and industrialized MFA bypass. The post frames AI as part of the attack surface, not just a tool attackers use.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description:
Microsoft describes a Linux-hosting intrusion technique where PHP webshells are triggered by specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post covers obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence used to hide activity and maintain access.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio got extensibility updates in the March 2026 release, including custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that improve agent capabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description:
GitHub has renamed the top-level Security tab to Security & quality across repositories, organizations, and enterprises on github.com. The navigation now groups code quality findings together with security-related information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says improved search for Issues is now generally available. It was previously in public preview and later added to the Issues dashboard.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization custom instructions are now generally available. Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise org admins can set default instructions for their organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to move to Windows Server 2025 before then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Mitigating the Axios npm supply chain compromise
item.description:
Microsoft reports that Axios was compromised in a March 31, 2026 npm supply chain attack. Two newly published version-update packages were used to download from command-and-control infrastructure, which Microsoft Threat Intelligence attributes to the North Korean actor Sapphire Sleet.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description:
Azure App Configuration now integrates with Azure Front Door in public preview, allowing dynamic configuration to be delivered directly to client-side applications at CDN scale. The update focuses on secure distribution and new flexibility for modern app configurations.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description:
Azure Data Box now automatically provides a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for each completed order. The certificate verifies the device data was securely erased according to NIST 800-88 Revision 2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files adds a QoS update for cool access on Premium and Ultra service levels. Throughput now adjusts automatically as data moves to cool storage, helping balance performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description:
Azure Data Box now supports ingesting data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 lets customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description:
Microsoft will retire eight Azure AI Language features on March 20, 2027. The notice lists Key Phrase Extraction, Sentiment Analysis and Opinion Mining, Custom Text Classification, Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), and other related language features.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise with data residency
item.description:
GitHub Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. Enterprises can use cloud development environments with configurable security while keeping data residency requirements in place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student users through Copilot auto model selection. It is included in Auto for GitHub Copilot Chat in supported clients such as Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now includes a refreshed Copilot tab, native session logs, and additional in-app controls for agent sessions. The update is aimed at making it easier to manage agentic workflows from the mobile app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues
item.description:
GitHub Mobile adds an Assign an Agent option in the issue overflow menu, making it faster to delegate work from an issue. The update is focused on quicker access to agent assignment and more flexible delegation from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description:
The page is an article introducing an agentic AI email bot built on AWS Bedrock AgentCore with Microsoft O365 integration. The provided excerpt is only a brief tagline and does not include implementation details.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Claude Sonnet 4 in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Claude Sonnet 4 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on 2026-05-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Machine Learning is retiring the Preview features for importing data from external sources and using external Data Connections. These features will stop being available after September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent, formerly Copilot coding agent, is no longer limited to pull-request workflows. This expands how it can be used for research, planning, and coding tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DevOps March 2026 updates
item.description:
Azure DevOps March 2026 updates include the Remote MCP Server entering public preview. The release also mentions security improvements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is adding Public Preview features for event-driven architectures, focused on improved interoperability, control, and security. The announced preview includes cross-tenant webhook delivery and related capabilities for managing large-scale distributed systems.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Dependabot now supports Xcode projects using SwiftPM with .xcodeproj manifests
item.description:
Dependabot now supports Swift package dependencies in Xcode projects that use .xcodeproj bundles to manage packages, even when there is no Package.swift file. It can detect and update those dependencies directly from the Xcode project manifest.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid has added several generally available capabilities to improve event-driven system management, interoperability, and control. The announced GA feature shown in the provided content is MQTT Message Ordering, which preserves in-order delivery of MQTT messages.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description:
User delegation SAS is now generally available for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues. It provides a more secure SAS option than account or service SAS by binding the token to the delegator.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure now available from new cloud region in Denmark
item.description:
Microsoft Azure has launched a new cloud region in Denmark, called Denmark East. It provides local cloud infrastructure for Danish customers with support for data residency and low-latency access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub secret scanning — coverage update
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning added nine new secret detectors from seven providers as part of a coverage update. The update includes detectors for providers such as Langchain, Salesforce, and Figma, along with additional secret types from Figma, Google, and OpenVSX.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: The threat to critical infrastructure has changed. Has your readiness?
item.description:
Microsoft says critical infrastructure threat patterns are changing and highlights five facts CI leaders should act on in 2026 based on current Microsoft Threat Intelligence observations across sectors. The post is a short security blog entry pointing readers to the full analysis.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: Manually registered Azure VPN Clients for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds will be retired on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Manually registered Azure VPN clients for point-to-site connections using Microsoft Entra ID authentication will be retired on March 31, 2029 in Azure Government and Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds. Users should take action to avoid disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in US Gov Arizona. The disk type is a next-generation block storage option for Azure VMs and is available in a region without Availability Zones.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Applying security fundamentals to AI: Practical advice for CISOs
item.description:
Microsoft Security published guidance for CISOs on securing AI systems by applying established security fundamentals to AI-powered environments. The post focuses on practical steps for managing AI risk rather than introducing new product features.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.0 adds Swift 6.2.4 support
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.0 has been released and updates Swift analysis support to version 6.2.4. This release affects GitHub code scanning users who analyze Swift code with CodeQL.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: WhatsApp malware campaign delivers VBS payloads and MSI backdoors
item.description:
Microsoft describes a malware campaign distributed through WhatsApp messages that uses VBS scripts to start a multi-stage infection chain. The attackers use renamed Windows tools and cloud-hosted payloads to deploy MSI backdoors and keep persistent access on infected systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: WhatsApp malware campaign delivers VBScript and MSI backdoors
item.description:
Microsoft describes a malware campaign delivered through WhatsApp messages that uses VBS scripts to start a multi-stage infection chain. The attackers rely on renamed Windows utilities and cloud-hosted payloads to install MSI backdoors and keep persistent access on compromised systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: CodeQL pull requests insights on security overview now cover all protected branches
item.description:
CodeQL pull request insights in GitHub Security Overview now include Copilot Autofix and alert statistics from all protected branches, instead of only the default branch.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: EU data residency region expanding to include EFTA countries
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com will expand the EU data residency region to include Azure infrastructure in EFTA countries, specifically Norway and Switzerland. This affects the EU data residency region for customers using GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching for VM/VMSS
item.description:
Azure VM and VMSS now support Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching in public preview. The OS disk image is cached entirely on local VM storage to improve OS disk performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in the Indonesia Central Azure region. This expands regional deployment options for customers running OpenShift workloads on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Deprecation of sidecar for remote-write for Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus
item.description:
Azure Monitor will deprecate the sidecar used for remote-write of Prometheus metrics to Azure Monitor Workspace on March 31, 2027. Microsoft recommends moving to a self-hosted configuration and notes the change is intended to improve reliability and reduce complexity.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired on March 31, 2029. Users must migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in South India. The region does not have Availability Zones, and the disk type provides sub-millisecond latency as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure VMs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Addressing the OWASP Top 10 Risks in Agentic AI with Microsoft Copilot Studio
item.description:
Microsoft published a security blog post mapping OWASP’s Top 10 risks for agentic applications to mitigations in Microsoft Copilot Studio. The article frames agentic AI as introducing new security risks and presents Copilot Studio as a way to address them.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Create issues from Slack with Copilot
item.description:
GitHub now lets you create Issues from Slack using the GitHub app and natural-language prompts. Mention @GitHub in a Slack channel, describe the work, and it will create the issue in GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36 Sneak Peek
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 is planned for April 22, 2026 and includes API deprecations/removals plus several feature graduations. Notable changes are deprecating Service .spec.externalIPs, disabling the gitRepo volume driver, and promoting SELinux volume labeling and external ServiceAccount token signing to GA.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: How Microsoft Defender protects high-value assets in real-world attack scenarios
item.description:
Microsoft says Defender uses Microsoft Security Exposure Management to apply asset-aware protection for high-value systems such as domain controllers, web servers, and identity infrastructure. The article describes real-world attack scenarios and defense techniques for those critical assets.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Gemini 3 Pro deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated Gemini 3 Pro across all GitHub Copilot experiences as of March 26, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Credential revocation API now supports GitHub OAuth and GitHub app credentials
item.description:
GitHub extended the Credential revocation API to support GitHub OAuth and GitHub App credentials. The API can now be used to programmatically revoke exposed credentials found in repositories or elsewhere.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to resolve merge conflicts on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot coding agent can now resolve merge conflicts on pull requests. Users can trigger it by mentioning @copilot in a PR comment and asking it to handle the conflicts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New pull requests dashboard is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has released a public preview of a refreshed pull requests dashboard at github.com/pulls. It adds a new pull request inbox and saved views for organizing and prioritizing pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View Agentic Workflow configs in the Actions run summary
item.description:
GitHub Actions run summaries now show Agentic Workflow markdown configs for agentic workflows. This makes it easier to review runs without leaving the summary page.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent activity in GitHub Issues and Projects
item.description:
GitHub added support for surfacing coding agent sessions in Issues and Projects, so agent activity can be tracked directly in planning workflows. The update includes agent sessions shown under assignees in GitHub Issues and agent activity integration in GitHub Projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-March 2026
item.description:
Late-March 2026 Azure SQL updates include support for configuring built-in SQL code analysis rules and severity settings without editing project XML, and new Fabric connectivity and provisioning options. The release note text is truncated, so only the listed items can be confirmed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Custom images for GitHub-hosted runners are now generally available
item.description:
Custom images for GitHub-hosted runners are now generally available. The feature, first released in public preview in October 2025, lets users build on GitHub-curated base images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements
item.description:
GitHub announced enhancements to the public preview of GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira based on early customer feedback. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Blue-green agent pool upgrade in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now offers a public preview of blue-green agent pool upgrades. The feature creates a parallel node pool with the new configuration so you can validate it before moving workloads, reducing risk from in-place upgrades.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric with private endpoints
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric now supports private endpoints and is generally available. This lets you run analytics on operational data while keeping network traffic private.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service: How we use your data
item.description:
GitHub updated its Privacy Statement and Terms of Service to clarify how it handles user data. The changes take effect on April 24.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active Copilot coding agent users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot coding agent (CCA) activity. Enterprise and organization admins can use this to identify active CCA users by day and other usage views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL
item.description:
Azure Database for MySQL – Flexible Server now has public preview support for Fabric Mirroring integration. It lets you replicate operational MySQL data into Microsoft Fabric in near real time without building or maintaining ETL pipelines.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom time zone support for pg_cron via cron.timezone in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports changing the cron.timezone server parameter for pg_cron. This lets scheduled jobs be evaluated and run in a specified time zone instead of the default.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Online migration now uses the pgoutput plugin
item.description:
Azure online PostgreSQL migrations now use the pgoutput plugin for generally available online (minimal-downtime) migration. The change is intended to improve reliability, performance, and compatibility with PostgreSQL logical replication.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports compatible EDB workloads into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL migration service now supports EDB PostgreSQL as a source, specifically EDB Postgres Extended Server. This enables migration and consolidation of compatible EDB PostgreSQL estates into Azure using secure, reliable workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports for Google AlloyDB into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL migration service now supports Google AlloyDB as a source. This enables migration and consolidation of PostgreSQL estates from AlloyDB into Azure using secure, reliable workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure SQL Managed Instance change event streaming
item.description:
Azure SQL Managed Instance now supports public preview of change event streaming (CES), which streams row-level inserts, updates, and deletes to Azure Event Hubs in near real time. Events are published as transactions commit to reduce latency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Disable comments on individual commits
item.description:
Repository admins can now disable comments on individual commits. GitHub added a new “Commits” section in repository settings to manage this behavior.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Identity security is the new pressure point for modern cyberattacks
item.description:
Microsoft points readers to its Secure Access report, which argues that identity has become a key attack surface and that a unified identity and access strategy improves protection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Guidance for detecting, investigating, and defending against the Trivy supply chain compromise
item.description:
Microsoft describes a Trivy supply-chain compromise in which attackers abused trusted distribution channels to deliver credential-stealing malware into CI/CD pipelines. The post outlines attacker techniques plus detection, investigation, and defense steps for security teams.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network metrics filtering for AKS
item.description:
Azure Container Networking Services (ACNS) for AKS now supports container network metrics filtering in general availability. This lets operators reduce metric volume and focus on operationally relevant data.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting
item.description:
Azure has a public preview of an AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting. It provides a lightweight web-based interface to help correlate logs and metrics for Kubernetes networking incidents.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
On April 21, 2026, we’re deprecating and removing the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to make changes to any pull request
item.description:
GitHub now lets you mention @copilot in any pull request to request changes directly in the PR. One announced use case is fixing failing GitHub Actions workflows and tests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage Copilot coding agent repository access via the API
item.description:
GitHub introduced public preview REST APIs for managing Copilot coding agent repository access programmatically at the organization level. This lets organization owners control access at scale through the API instead of only manual settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AKS managed GPU metrics in Azure Monitor
item.description:
AKS public preview adds managed GPU metrics for NVIDIA GPU-enabled node pools. The metrics are exposed automatically into managed Prometheus, giving teams GPU performance and utilization visibility alongside Kubernetes metrics.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Cross-cluster networking in Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager is adding cross-cluster networking in public preview. The update targets networking across multiple Kubernetes clusters to improve global service discovery, performance, and observability in distributed microservice environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 now with Elastic SAN integration and on demand installation
item.description:
Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 is generally available and adds Elastic SAN integration. It lets Kubernetes clusters use storage from a shared pool and supports on-demand installation.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network logs in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) container network logs are now generally available. The feature adds visibility into pod/network traffic to help diagnose Kubernetes networking issues and failures.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Application routing with meshless Istio in AKS
item.description:
Azure AKS preview introduces Application Routing with meshless Istio as a supported ingress migration path after ingress-nginx deprecation, without requiring a full service mesh. It is positioned around Kubernetes Gateway API support and Istio-based routing.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network is in public preview. It targets Kubernetes environments spread across regions and clusters by adding application-layer networking controls and visibility beyond IP-based networking.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Prometheus community recommended alerts for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes
item.description:
Azure Monitor now lets you enable Prometheus recommended alerts with one click in the Azure Portal for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. The alerts are based on enhanced Prometheus community rules and expand monitoring coverage for cluster scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingest OTLP data into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description:
Azure Monitor now has public preview support for native OTLP ingestion. You can send telemetry from OpenTelemetry-instrumented apps and platforms directly to Azure Monitor using the OpenTelemetry Collector.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Governing AI agent behavior: Aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent
item.description:
The report discusses how to govern AI agent behavior by aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent for secure enterprise AI adoption.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Faster incremental analysis with CodeQL in pull requests
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C#, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and Ruby are now incremental, which improves scan speed. GitHub had already sped up pull request scans earlier this year.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse
item.description:
Gemini 3.1 Pro is now in public preview for Copilot Enterprise, Business, Pro, and Pro+ users. It can be accessed in GitHub Copilot Chat and in JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and Eclipse.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions from repository settings
item.description:
GitHub now lets repository admins manage secret scanning push protection exemptions from a repository’s settings. Before this change, exemptions were only configurable through organization- and enterprise-level security configurations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Generative AI for Beginners .NET: Version 2 on .NET 10
item.description:
Microsoft announced Version 2 of Generative AI for Beginners .NET, a free course rebuilt for .NET 10. It adds Microsoft.Extensions.AI, updated RAG patterns, and new agent framework content across five lessons focused on production-ready AI apps.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Case study: How predictive shielding in Defender stopped GPO-based ransomware before it started
item.description:
Microsoft Defender blocked a human-operated ransomware attack that used Group Policy Objects (GPOs) to disable defenses and deploy encryption at scale. Predictive shielding hardened 700 devices in time, preventing any GPO-based encryptions and blocking most of the attempted impact.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: Ten Months with Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime
item.description:
The .NET team reports on ten months of using GitHub Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime and summarizes lessons from cloud-AI-assisted development. The post is a data-driven retrospective rather than a feature release.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL preview updates in mid-March 2026 add changes aimed at reducing storage, I/O, and memory use while improving performance without requiring index maintenance jobs. The provided note is truncated and does not include the full list of enhancements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode
item.description:
Azure SQL Elastic Query using the Shard_Map_Manager external data source type reaches end of support on March 31, 2027. Existing workloads can keep running, but this mode will no longer receive updates or support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Announcing Ingress2Gateway 1.0: Your Path to Gateway API
item.description:
Ingress2Gateway 1.0 is a stable migration assistant for moving from Ingress to Gateway API, with expanded Ingress-NGINX annotation support and more detailed warnings for unsupported or lossy translations. The release emphasizes controller-level integration tests and safer migration workflows, including manual review, validation in a dev cluster, and gradual traffic shifting.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now resolve auto model selection to actual models
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics now map activity that was previously shown as “Auto” to the actual model used when auto model selection is enabled. This gives clearer model-level reporting in Copilot usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Running Agents on Kubernetes with Agent Sandbox
item.description:
Kubernetes SIG Apps is developing Agent Sandbox, a new API for singleton, stateful AI agent workloads that need persistent identity, isolation, and suspend/resume behavior. The project adds a Sandbox CRD plus extensions like warm pools to reduce cold starts and support untrusted code execution.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
item.description:
Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031. After that date, Microsoft will stop providing customer application, OS, bug, and security updates, as well as DAA certificate issuance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: CTI-REALM: A new benchmark for end-to-end detection rule generation with AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft introduced CTI-REALM, an open-source benchmark for evaluating AI agents on end-to-end detection engineering. It measures whether agents can turn cyber threat intelligence into validated detection rules.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Secure agentic AI end-to-end
item.description:
Microsoft announced new security capabilities for the agentic AI era at RSAC 2026, focused on securing agents, securing AI foundations, and using agents alongside experts for defense. The post frames these as purpose-built additions to cover the AI estate end to end.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Trace any Copilot coding agent commit to its session logs
item.description:
GitHub says commits made by Copilot coding agent are authored by Copilot with the assigning human listed as co-author, and that these commits can now be traced back to the agent session logs. This is meant to make it easier to inspect how a given commit was produced.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Monitor Copilot coding agent logs live in Raycast
item.description:
GitHub says the Copilot extension for Raycast can now be used to monitor Copilot coding agent logs live. The post links Raycast’s launcher and extension support with GitHub Copilot agent log viewing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: A smoother navigation experience in GitHub Mobile for Android
item.description:
GitHub Mobile for Android has updated its navigation to be more consistent and intuitive across the app. The change focuses on smoother movement between key areas like Home and other main sections.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub added session log visibility for Copilot coding agent tasks. You can now review what the agent did while it worked in the background before approving its changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents
item.description:
Microsoft says custom-built AI agents are being used to improve the .NET MAUI contribution workflow, with reported 50–70% reductions in issue resolution time. The post also claims increased test coverage and code quality.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Late March 2026 updates
item.description:
GitHub Actions added support for time zones in scheduled workflows and the ability to use environments without automatic deployments. This release focuses on removing a couple of workflow configuration limitations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions Runner Controller release 0.14.0
item.description:
Actions Runner Controller (ARC) 0.14.0 is generally available. The release adds multilabel support for runner scale sets, switches to the actions/scaleset library client, and introduces resource customization options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster. The update applies when handing work to the agent through supported entry points such as assigning an issue to Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: New tools and guidance: Announcing Zero Trust for AI
item.description:
Microsoft added a new AI pillar to its Zero Trust materials, including an updated workshop, reference architecture, guidance, and a new assessment tool. The announcement focuses on applying Zero Trust principles to AI systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Codespaces with data residency now available in Japan
item.description:
GitHub Codespaces with data residency now supports Japan as a region. This adds Japan to the existing EU and Australia data residency regions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks OneLake Catalog Federation
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports OneLake catalog federation in public preview. It lets Unity Catalog query Microsoft Fabric OneLake data without copying it, while automatically syncing schemas and tables from the connected OneLake catalog.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HBv2-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HBv2-series VM sizes on May 31, 2027. The affected sizes are Standard_HB120rs_v2, Standard_HB120-96rs_v2, Standard_HB120-64rs_v2, Standard_HB120-32rs_v2, and Standard_HB120-16rs_v2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HC-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HC-series VM sizes Standard_HC44rs, Standard_HC44-16rs, and Standard_HC44-32rs on May 31, 2027. Users should plan to migrate to a current supported VM size before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: NP-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire NP-series VM sizes Standard_NP10s, Standard_NP20s, and Standard_NP40s on May 31, 2027. Users should migrate workloads to alternative Azure GPU VM sizes before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out docked panels on pull request “Files changed” pages so reviewers can keep pull request context open side-by-side while reviewing code. The panels include overview, comments, and merge status.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available
item.description:
Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available. It is enabled by default for all new project views, and existing views can be updated to use it.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: When tax season becomes cyberattack season: Phishing and malware campaigns using tax-related lures
item.description:
Microsoft says threat actors are using tax-season urgency to deliver phishing and malware via refund notices, payroll forms, filing reminders, and fake tax-professional requests. The lures can include malicious attachments, links, and QR codes.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now automatically runs project tests and linting, plus GitHub validation tools such as CodeQL, advisory database checks, and secret scanning. The new update lets you configure which validation tools the agent uses.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description:
The Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure, hosted by Power BI, will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, it will no longer be accessible and technical support will end.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL received mid-March 2026 updates including direct publishing of SQL database projects from Visual Studio Code and the ability to view and edit SQL table data directly in MSSQ.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift now generally supports managed identities and workload identities. This lets clusters and applications run on Azure without long-lived service principal credentials.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects
item.description:
SQL Database Projects are now in public preview in SSMS, bringing schema-as-code workflows for database development. The preview supports source-controlled schemas, repeatable deployments, and code quality checks in the development process.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds a public preview of GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer. This expands the visual schema design workflow with AI-assisted help based on developer feedback.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
Azure announced a public preview of SQL MCP Server, an MCP connector for production databases. It is part of Data API Builder and is intended to provide a simple, predictable, and secure way to connect AI agents to data workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22. It adds AI-powered assistance inside SSMS, including natural-language help to develop, explain, and fix SQL-related tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Securing Production Debugging in Kubernetes
item.description:
The post recommends securing production Kubernetes debugging with least-privilege RBAC, short-lived identity-bound credentials, and a just-in-time access gateway instead of shared bastions, cluster-admin access, or long-lived SSH keys. It outlines practical patterns for namespace-scoped roles, group-based bindings, OIDC or client-certificate auth, and gateway-mediated sessions with audit logging and automatic expiration.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It provides a guided experience for generating backend APIs directly in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks has launched a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier in Azure Databricks. Each workspace gets 100 free DBUs per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases, which is described as supporting roughly 100 million records per workspace per day.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Versionless key support for transparent data encryption in Azure SQL Database
item.description:
Azure SQL Database now supports versionless keys for transparent data encryption (TDE). This removes the need to reference a specific key version in Azure Key Vault or Managed HSM, simplifying customer-managed key setup and reducing maintenance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex long-term support in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub introduced GPT-5.3-Codex as a long-term support (LTS) model for GitHub Copilot, aimed at giving enterprises a stable option for internal security and safety reviews. The LTS commitment is available for Copilot Business and Copilot plans.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Observability for AI Systems: Strengthening visibility for proactive risk detection
item.description:
The post says observability is becoming necessary as AI systems become more autonomous, because visibility into AI behavior can help detect risk earlier and support secure development. It is presented as a Microsoft Security Blog article focused on proactive risk detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: aks: Release Notes - 2026-03-05
item.description:
AKS released new Kubernetes patch versions, updated several components and node images, and introduced/expanded features including OTLP gRPC support for Azure Monitor Profile, GA auto-instrumentation, and broader Azure Linux GPU and KAITO support. It also announced deprecations and behavior changes, including retirement dates for Flatcar preview support and deprecation of Kubernetes LTS 1.28.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search
item.description:
Copilot coding agent now supports a semantic code search tool, so it can find relevant code by meaning instead of exact text matches. GitHub says this should make the agent faster when working in codebases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now detects malware in npm dependencies
item.description:
Dependabot can now alert on npm dependencies that match known malware advisories. When malware alerting is enabled, it checks repository npm dependencies against malicious package versions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning in AI coding agents via the GitHub MCP Server
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server can now scan code changes for exposed secrets before commit or pull request creation. The feature is intended to catch credential leaks early in AI coding agent workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is generally available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with an improved merge experience among the highlighted changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Default Rule Set 2.2 and updates to ruleset support policy
item.description:
Azure WAF has made Default Rule Set (DRS) 2.2 generally available on Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. The managed ruleset support policy is being updated to reflect this release and provide more predictable support guidance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of the next-generation Foundry Agent Service. It introduces a redesigned API format and runtime experience for building and operating agents from prototype to production.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description:
Microsoft will retire Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 nodes on June 30, 2029. Existing AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance terms are not affected, but customers should review RI expirations and plan migration before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available in GitHub Copilot and is rolling out to users. GitHub says early tests show it as OpenAI’s highest-performing mini agentic coding model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity. Organization admins can view CLI-specific activity and usage totals in 1-day usage metrics, completing telemetry coverage across enterprise, user, and organization levels.

  • Source: GitHub
