item.title: dotnet: RT.Assistant: A Multi-Agent Voice Bot Using .NET and OpenAI
item.description:
The post describes RT.Assistant, a real-time multi-agent voice bot built with .NET, F#, Microsoft.Extensions.AI, .NET MAUI, and the OpenAI Realtime API. It is presented as a guest blog post on the .NET Blog.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions for roles, teams, and apps
item.description:
GitHub added push protection exemptions for secret scanning at the organization level. Specific roles, teams, and GitHub Apps can now be exempted, with exemption status checked on each push.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Advanced Security setup made simple
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security now has a guided setup experience for organizations. It lets admins set up and configure Advanced Security more easily, including editing configurations and repository settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role
item.description:
GitHub changed repository permissions for Code Quality: security managers can no longer enable or disable GitHub Code Quality unless they are also repository administrators. The ability to toggle Code Quality is now limited to repository admins.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Batch apply quality suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality findings on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for Code Quality findings in the Files changed tab by…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: The Invisible Rewrite: Modernizing the Kubernetes Image Promoter
item.description:
Kubernetes rewrote the core of its image promoter (kpromo/promo-tools), removing legacy code and splitting promotion into distinct phases. The new pipeline improved performance and reliability, shipped in v4.2.0–v4.4.0, and kept user-facing flags and manifests unchanged.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: ai: Foundry Agent Service is GA: private networking, Voice Live, and enterprise-grade evaluations
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service is now generally available with several new production features, including end-to-end private networking, Voice Live integration, expanded MCP authentication, GA evaluations with continuous monitoring, and hosted agent deployments in six additional Azure regions.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migration of Azure Batch Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs in early March
item.description:
Azure Batch will begin a system-initiated migration from Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs starting March 1, 2026. Low-Priority VMs were retired on September 30, 2025, and the change aligns Batch with Azure Spot infrastructure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Azure Batch pools will end on January 12, 2027
item.description:
Azure Batch will stop supporting Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Batch pools on January 12, 2027. This follows the end of extended support for Windows Server 2016.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Entra ID-Based Access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description:
Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now in public preview. It lets you use Entra ID identities, including guest users through Entra External Identities, to connect securely to Azure Blob Storage via SFTP.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will end support for NVv3 and NVv4-series virtual machines in Azure Batch pools on September 30, 2026. The notice lists affected NVv3 sizes, including Standard_NV12s_v3, NV12hs_v3, NV24s_v3, NV24ms_v3, NV32ms_v3, and NV48s_v3.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS in preview is being retired on June 8, 2026. Users should move to a supported alternative by that date; the preview remains usable until June 7, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: New Microsoft Purview innovations for Fabric to safely accelerate your AI transformation
item.description:
Microsoft announced new Microsoft Purview innovations for Fabric aimed at supporting safer AI adoption by strengthening security and governance. The post frames Purview as part of the governance layer for organizations using AI with Fabric.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Help on the line: How a Microsoft Teams support call led to compromise
item.description:
Microsoft describes a DART investigation into a Teams voice-phishing attack that used deception and trusted Microsoft tools to enable an identity-led intrusion. The post focuses on how the compromise happened and how to prevent similar attacks.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page
item.description:
We are deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page and in the People/Licensing CSV export. When the cost center integration was originally shipped in 2023, cost center…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: devops+ai: AgentCore: Deployment, Operations, and Lessons Learned (4/4) 🔥
item.description:
The provided content is only a short teaser and does not include substantive release or changelog details. It appears to be a post about AgentCore deployment, operations, and lessons learned, but no具体 information is available from the excerpt.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: Optionally skip approval for Copilot coding agent Actions workflows
item.description:
When Copilot coding agent opens a pull request or pushes changes, Copilot is treated like an outside contributor in an open source project. GitHub Actions workflows do not run until…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Self-hosted runner minimum version enforcement paused
item.description:
Editor’s note (March 17, 2026): We updated this post to clarify that the v2.329.0 minimum version enforcement that has been paused applies only at configuration/registration time (i.e., when running ./config.sh…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot for students
item.description:
Starting today, students with GitHub Education benefits are now on the new GitHub Copilot Student plan. As part of this transition, we’ve updated the available model lineup. The new GitHub…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover enables private data transfers from AWS S3 to Azure Blob
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now in public preview for private transfers from AWS S3 in a Virtual Private Cloud to Azure Blob Storage. It supports secure private networking and automation, reducing the need for manual pipelines or third-party tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Summary rules in Log Analytics workspace now support manual “Retry bin”
item.description:
Log Analytics workspace summary rules now support a manual “Retry bin” action in generally available release. This lets you rerun failed bins to fill gaps in summarized data.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Modernize .NET Anywhere with GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the modernize-dotnet agent to help assess apps, generate upgrade plans, and modernize .NET projects. It is available in Visual Studio, VS Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and GitHub.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 10.0.5 Out-of-Band Release – macOS Debugger Fix
item.description:
Microsoft released .NET 10.0.5 as an out-of-band update to fix a critical debugger crash on macOS when using Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Storm-2561 uses SEO poisoning to distribute fake VPN clients for credential theft
item.description:
Microsoft reports that Storm-2561 has been using SEO poisoning to promote fake VPN client downloads that install signed trojans and steal VPN credentials. The activity has been observed since 2025 and relies on impersonating trusted brands and abusing legitimate services.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: REST API version 2026-03-10 is now available
item.description:
Previously, we introduced calendar-based versioning for our REST API, giving us a path to evolving our API while giving integrators plenty of time and clear guidance for upgrading. Now, we’re…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: From transparency to action: What the latest Microsoft email security benchmark reveals
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post about its latest email security benchmarking data, comparing Microsoft Defender’s mitigation of modern email threats against SEG and ICES vendors. The post says the benchmark is intended to move from transparency to action.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot auto model selection is generally available in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in JetBrains IDEs for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses a model on your behalf based on real-time model availability…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions OIDC tokens now support repository custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Actions OpenID Connect (OIDC) tokens now support repository custom properties as claims. Additionally, a new settings page is available in public preview, making it easy to configure OIDC token…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Detecting and analyzing prompt abuse in AI tools
item.description:
Microsoft describes how hidden instructions in content can influence AI tool behavior and uses a scenario to illustrate prompt injection. The post emphasizes the need for human oversight and a structured response playbook.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Issue fields: Structured issue metadata is in public preview
item.description:
Issue fields are now available in public preview for select GitHub organizations. If you’ve been using labels like priority/p0 or severity/high to track structured data in issues, you know the…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Contagious Interview: Malware delivered through fake developer job interviews
item.description:
Microsoft describes the Contagious Interview campaign, where attackers impersonate recruiters from crypto and AI firms to target developers with fake coding assessments. The delivered malware installs backdoors and steals API tokens, cloud credentials, crypto wallets, and source code.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Request Copilot code review from GitHub CLI
item.description:
You can now request a review from GitHub Copilot directly from your terminal using the GitHub CLI. Whether you’re editing an existing pull request or creating a new one, Copilot…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Customer‑managed encryption keys now supported on Premium SSD v2 disks for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports customer-managed keys (CMKs) with Premium SSD v2 disks in public preview. This lets you control encryption of data at rest for workloads using these disks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL dashboards with Grafana
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now has built-in Grafana dashboards in the Azure portal. The integration uses Azure Monitor, so you do not need a separate Grafana instance to view metrics.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Terraform, Bicep, Ansible support for elastic clusters on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL elastic clusters now have General Availability support for provisioning and management through Terraform, Bicep, and Ansible. This adds native infrastructure-as-code options for creating, scaling, and managing elastic clusters on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Query Profiler in MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now has Query Profiler in public preview. It lets you capture query and database activity and view it in real time within VS Code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SRE Agent with new capabilities
item.description:
Azure SRE Agent is now generally available. The GA release adds new capabilities focused on incident diagnosis, automated response workflows, and improving uptime while reducing operational toil.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Major agentic capabilities improvements in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
This update brings several new features and improvements to GitHub Copilot in JetBrains IDEs. Core agentic capabilities, including custom agents, sub-agents, and plan agent, are now generally available, with agent…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Explore a repository using Copilot on the web
item.description:
We’ve made it easier to explore a repository’s file structure when you chat with Copilot on the web. What’s changed Developers can now ask Copilot to show a file, then…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates — March 2026
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning continually updates its detectors, validators, and analyzers. Here’s what’s new for March 2026. 28 new secret detectors from 15 providers, including Lark, Vercel, Snowflake, and Supabase. 39…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 2 is now available!
item.description:
Microsoft announced .NET 11 Preview 2, with updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, Blazor, C#, and .NET MAUI. The post points readers to the preview release details, but the provided excerpt does not list specific changes.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework March 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
This post recaps the March 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. It is a brief announcement and does not include technical details in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.24.3 adds Java 26 support and other improvements
item.description:
CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.24.3, which adds support for Java 26…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now supports pre-commit hooks
item.description:
GitHub Dependabot now natively supports automatic dependency updates for pre-commit hooks. By adding pre-commit as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml configuration, Dependabot will parse your .pre-commit-config.yaml, check each hook’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Extend your coding agent with .NET Skills
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the dotnet/skills repository, describing a set of .NET “agent skills” intended to extend coding agents and improve agent-driven development workflows.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: k8s: Announcing the AI Gateway Working Group
item.description:
Kubernetes has formed a new AI Gateway Working Group to define standards and best practices for Gateway API-based networking infrastructure tailored to AI workloads.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: Secure agentic AI for your Frontier Transformation
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog post about securing agentic AI initiatives, highlighting Microsoft Agent 365 and Microsoft 365 E7 in the context of a “Frontier Transformation.”

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | February 2026
item.description:
February 2026 updates add new/updated foundation models (Claude 4.6, GPT Realtime/Audio 1.5, Grok 4.0 GA), advance the Microsoft Agent Framework to Release Candidate, and move Foundry REST API v1 to GA.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Figma MCP server can now generate design layers from VS Code
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in VS Code can now connect to the Figma MCP server to use Figma design context while coding and to export rendered UI back to Figma as editable frames.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code v1.110 – February release
item.description:
Changelog entry for GitHub Copilot in VS Code v1.110 (February 2026) indicates improvements that make Copilot agents more practical for longer-running, more complex tasks, with more control over agent execution and new extensibility options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: AI as tradecraft: How threat actors operationalize AI
item.description:
Microsoft reports that threat actors are using AI to scale and sustain malicious operations, accelerating tradecraft and increasing defender risk. It cites recent activity involving North Korean groups Jasper Sleet and Coral Sleet (formerly Storm-1877).

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 is generally available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out general availability of the GPT-5.4 model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Discover and manage agent activity with new session filters
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise AI Controls and the agent control plane add new session filters to help enterprises find and manage agent activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pick a model for @copilot in pull request comments
item.description:
GitHub Copilot’s coding agent can be invoked in pull request comments via @copilot, and the feature adds the ability to choose which model @copilot uses for that request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hierarchy view improvements and file uploads in issue forms
item.description:
GitHub Projects hierarchy view gained new improvements, including the ability to filter sub-issues using syntax. GitHub issue forms also added support for file uploads.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot coding agent can now be assigned Jira issues to asynchronously generate draft pull requests in a linked GitHub repository. This integration is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review now runs on an agentic architecture
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review has moved to an agentic, tool-calling architecture and is now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Quick access to merge status in pull requests is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a merge-status indicator at the top of pull request pages, now in public preview. This lets reviewers and authors check merge readiness from anywhere in the PR view.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Release v1.0 of the official MCP C# SDK
item.description:
MCP C# SDK v1.0 focuses on improved authorization handling, expanded metadata support, and added patterns for tool calling and long-running operations.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Women’s History Month: Encouraging women in cybersecurity at every career stage
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog post for Women’s History Month focusing on how to support and encourage women in cybersecurity across different career stages.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Add images to agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub agent sessions now support starting from an image by adding it directly in the agent UI on github.com.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Malicious AI Assistant Extensions Harvest LLM Chat Histories
item.description:
Microsoft reports a campaign of malicious AI browser extensions that exfiltrated LLM chat histories and browsing data from services including ChatGPT and DeepSeek, affecting large numbers of users and enterprises.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes user-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include user-level activity for GitHub Copilot CLI, expanding beyond prior enterprise-level CLI telemetry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — February update
item.description:
The February update to Visual Studio 2026 brings expanded agent capabilities, smarter code completions, and deeper Copilot integration across debugging, testing, and modernization workflows in Visual Studio. Highlights Here’s what’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: vscode: Making agents practical for real-world development
item.description:
VS Code 1.110 describes changes aimed at making coding agents more usable in real workflows, focusing on orchestration, extensibility, and session continuity.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: github: Lock and unlock draft repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added the ability for repository administrators to lock draft repository security advisories and private vulnerability reports to stop collaborators from editing them.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Policy faster enforcement and retirement of login/logout workaround
item.description:
Azure Policy now enforces new and updated Resource Manager mode policy assignments within 5 minutes. As a result, the previously recommended login/logout workaround (used to refresh cached policy state) is being retired.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Grok Code Fast 1 is now available in Copilot Free auto model selection
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Free’s auto model selection now includes Grok Code Fast 1, which is generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: vscode: February 2026 Insiders (version 1.110)
item.description:
Insufficient information to summarize: only the article title, URL, and a generic “learn what is new” line were provided, without release note details.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: security: Inside Tycoon2FA: How a leading AiTM phishing kit operated at scale
item.description:
Microsoft reports that the Tycoon2FA adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing-as-a-service kit enabled large-scale phishing campaigns and says it coordinated a disruption of the kit’s infrastructure with Europol and industry partners.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot Memory now on by default for Pro and Pro+ users in public preview
item.description:
Copilot Memory is now enabled by default for GitHub Copilot Pro and Pro+ users in public preview, instead of being opt-in.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alert assignees are now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Dependabot now supports assigning vulnerability alerts to specific users (generally available). This adds per-alert ownership to help teams track and remediate dependency vulnerabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Signed malware impersonating workplace apps deploys RMM backdoors
item.description:
Signed malware using a stolen EV code-signing certificate impersonated workplace apps to install legitimate remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools for persistent enterprise access. The post advises tightening certificate controls and monitoring RMM tool usage to reduce risk.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality enterprise policy
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security enterprise policies now allow configuring GitHub Code Quality availability independently from Code Security.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: devops+ai: AWS AgentCore Agentic Slack Bot - AgentCore Memory and Tools🔥
item.description:
The provided content contains only a short tagline and no release notes or changelog details to summarize.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: Email notifications for included usage thresholds
item.description:
GitHub now sends email alerts as an account’s included monthly usage approaches its thresholds.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Draft & Deploy on Azure Firewall
item.description:
Azure Firewall Policy adds a Draft & Deploy workflow that separates editing from deployment to reduce deployment time and operational disruption when updating policies.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5.1 models
item.description:
GitHub Copilot will deprecate the Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5.1 models across all Copilot experiences on a specified deprecation date, with replacement models recommended.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Network configuration changes for Copilot coding agent now in effect
item.description:
GitHub has put into effect (as of Feb 27, 2026) previously announced required network configuration changes for teams running Copilot coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: OAuth redirection abuse enables phishing and malware delivery
item.description:
Microsoft reports attackers abusing OAuth redirect behavior to move users from legitimate sign-in pages to attacker-controlled sites, enabling phishing and malware delivery via trusted authentication flows.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Lakebase
item.description:
Azure Databricks Lakebase is now generally available as a managed PostgreSQL service designed for OLTP with separated storage and compute.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks update workspace network configuration
item.description:
Azure Databricks now generally supports updating an existing workspace’s network configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot metrics now includes plan mode
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include telemetry for plan mode. Enterprises can track plan mode adoption and engagement alongside existing Copilot metrics.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot metrics reports now return consistent usernames for Enterprise Managed Users
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics reports now return a consistent user_login value for Enterprise Managed Users. This addresses cases where reports previously included a suffixed user_login, complicating analysis.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in New Zealan North
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in the New Zealand North region.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VMs
item.description:
Azure announced general availability of the DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VM series based on 5th Gen Intel Xeon processors with Intel Trust Domain Extensions (TDX).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Managed NGINX Ingress with Application Routing Add-on Retiring November 2026
item.description:
Microsoft will retire the Managed NGINX Ingress used by the AKS Application Routing add-on in November 2026 due to the upstream Ingress-NGINX project deprecating and ending updates after March 2026. Microsoft will provide only critical security patches until 30 Nov 2026, after which the controller is unsupported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes enterprise-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot enterprise usage metrics now include GitHub Copilot CLI telemetry, expanding what’s counted in enterprise-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now available in Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in three additional Azure regions: Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot metrics is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics are now generally available, providing a centralized view of team adoption and usage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Before You Migrate: Five Surprising Ingress-NGINX Behaviors You Need to Know
item.description:
Kubernetes will retire the community Ingress-NGINX controller in March 2026; this post lists five non-obvious routing/rewriting/redirect behaviors that can cause outages when translating to Gateway API unless explicitly replicated.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Actions now supports uploading and downloading non-zipped artifacts
item.description:
GitHub Actions artifacts can now be uploaded and downloaded without automatic ZIP compression in workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot metrics report URLs update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API download URLs are now served from a new endpoint; report data, API contract, and response schema are unchanged.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise AI Controls & agent control plane now generally available
item.description:
GitHub announced general availability of Enterprise AI Controls and the agent control plane for GitHub Enterprise administrators.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Restrict usage of user delegation SAS to an Entra ID identity
item.description:
Azure Storage adds a preview feature to restrict (bind) user delegation SAS usage to a specific Microsoft Entra ID identity, strengthening authentication by tying SAS access to the user.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Vector Data in .NET – Building Blocks for AI Part 2
item.description:
Microsoft describes Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData as a .NET library that standardizes access to vector databases for semantic search scenarios.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Claude and Codex now available for Copilot Business & Pro users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Business and Copilot Pro customers can now use Anthropic Claude and OpenAI Codex as coding agents. Copilot Enterprise and Pro+ customers got access earlier in February 2026.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Threat modeling AI applications
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog notes that AI threat modeling is used to identify misuse, emergent risks, and failure modes in probabilistic and agentic AI systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: macos-26 is now generally available for GitHub-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions’ macOS 26 runner image is now generally available for GitHub-hosted runners, moving out of public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Content Exclusion REST API in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot adds a new Content Exclusion REST API (JSON) in public preview for organization and enterprise admins to manage Copilot content exclusion rules programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Track coding agent progress in real time with Live Notifications
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports Live Coding Agent Notifications to monitor Copilot coding agent activity and supported third-party agent sessions in real time from the mobile app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search on the issues dashboard
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out semantic (natural-language) search in public preview on the Issues dashboard over the coming week.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure ingestion and pod placement for Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline adds public-preview support for secure ingestion from external endpoints using TLS/mTLS and Bring Your Own Certificates (BYOC), plus new controls for pod placement.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Org-level metrics API now includes pull request throughput metric parity
item.description:
GitHub expanded the organization-level Copilot Usage Metrics API to add pull request activity metrics, bringing the org-level endpoints closer to other metrics parity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: vscode: Building Long-Distance Next Edit Suggestions
item.description:
VS Code describes extending GitHub Copilot Next Edit Suggestions so they can propose edits across an entire file rather than only nearby locations.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: github: Generate pull request titles with Copilot on the web
item.description:
GitHub Copilot can now generate pull request titles directly on github.com when creating or editing a PR.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now available in github.com, GitHub Mobile, and Visual Studio
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is generally available for GitHub Copilot paid tiers and can be used via Copilot Chat across github.com, GitHub Mobile, and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved web search in Copilot on github.com
item.description:
Copilot Chat on github.com now supports model-native web search for certain models, allowing those models to use their built-in web browsing capability during chats.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description:
Late-February 2026 Azure SQL updates add a database object search feature to the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description:
Late-February 2026 preview updates for Azure SQL add in-dialog database management actions and introduce flat-file import support in the MSSQL extension.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Geo‑redundant backups for Premium SSD v2 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports configuring geo-redundant automated backups when using Premium SSD v2 disks (public preview). This adds a disaster-recovery option by storing backups in a geo-redundant manner.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot CLI is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI is now generally available for all paid GitHub Copilot subscribers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 release candidate is available. The changelog post indicates improvements in deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, but provides no concrete details in the provided excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: The Dongle Died at Midnight – WinForms Agent Saved my German Mom’s Business Trip
item.description:
A .NET Blog post describing how the author used a WinForms agent/AI-assisted development workflow to quickly build a replacement time-study app after a hardware dongle failed during a business trip.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: CodeQL adds Go 1.26 and Kotlin 2.3.10 support and improves query accuracy
item.description:
CodeQL 2.24.2 adds support for Go 1.26 and Kotlin 2.3.10, and includes query accuracy improvements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot can group updates by dependency name across multiple directories
item.description:
Dependabot can now group update PRs by dependency name across multiple directories in a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Organization-level dashboard in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality added an organization-level dashboard in public preview to view code health across repositories with Code Quality enabled.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository dashboard is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub’s repository dashboard has moved from public preview to general availability. It lets users find, filter, and save custom views of repositories they can access.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Developer-targeting campaign using malicious Next.js repositories
item.description:
Microsoft reports a developer-targeting campaign that used malicious Next.js repositories to achieve remote code execution and establish command-and-control via normal build workflows, aiming to blend into routine development activity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Scaling security operations with Microsoft Defender autonomous defense and expert-led services
item.description:
Guide-style post arguing that manual SOC processes and legacy tools can’t keep up with AI-enabled attacks, and positioning Microsoft Defender’s autonomous capabilities plus expert-led services as the proposed approach.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Enterprise-defined custom organization roles are generally available
item.description:
GitHub now generally makes available enterprise-defined custom organization roles to help enterprises standardize role-based access across multiple organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Windows Server Annual Channel (preview)
item.description:
Windows Server Annual Channel (Preview) on AKS will retire on May 15, 2026. Users should migrate to Windows Server Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: IP allow list coverage extended to EMU namespaces in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports extending GitHub’s native IP allow list to user namespaces for Enterprise Managed Users (EMUs), available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise team support in organization APIs
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise organization owners can now discover and manage both enterprise teams and organization teams via existing organization team API endpoints.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: WAF Insights for Application Gateway
item.description:
Azure Application Gateway WAF Insights is now in public preview, adding an interactive view of WAF logs and metrics. It is intended to help investigate blocked requests and analyze attack patterns.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Organization-level Copilot usage metrics dashboard available in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added an organization-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics dashboard in public preview, expanding access beyond enterprise-only dashboards.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in Brazil Southeast, and in a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 disks are now generally available in Brazil Southeast and expanded to a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central.

  • Source: Azure Updates
